questions.question: What is a line of symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: An imaginary line where you can fold a figure so that both halves match exactly
questions.explanation: If a shape has a line of symmetry, one half is the mirror image of the other.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon has as many lines of symmetry as the number of its sides.

questions.question: What is rotational symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: When a figure looks the same after a rotation of less than 360 degrees
questions.explanation: The figure fits onto itself during a partial turn around a center point.

questions.question: What is the 'Order of Rotational Symmetry'?
questions.correct_answer: The number of times a figure looks exactly the same in a full 360-degree turn
questions.explanation: A square has an order of 4 because it looks the same at 90, 180, 270, and 360 degrees.

questions.question: What is the center of rotation?
questions.correct_answer: The fixed point around which a shape is turned
questions.explanation: In a wheel, the axle is the center of rotation.

questions.question: What is the angle of rotation for an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: Calculated as 360 divided by 3 (the number of sides).

questions.question: Does a parallelogram have any lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: While it has rotational symmetry, it cannot be folded into two matching halves.

questions.question: Which letter of the alphabet has both horizontal and vertical line symmetry?
questions.explanation: These letters can be folded both ways to match perfectly.

questions.question: What is the common ground between a circle and a square regarding symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: Both have line symmetry and rotational symmetry
questions.explanation: However, a circle has infinite lines of symmetry while a square has four.

questions.question: Where is symmetry used in architecture?
questions.correct_answer: To create balance and aesthetic appeal in buildings
questions.explanation: Symmetry provides structural stability and visual harmony.

notes.title: Reflection Symmetry
notes.content: Another name for line symmetry, as it acts like a mirror reflection.

notes.title: Order 1 Symmetry
notes.content: Every object looks the same after 360 degrees, so 'Order 1' effectively means no rotational symmetry.

notes.title: Regular Polygons
notes.content: They are the 'champions' of symmetry, having equal lines and order of rotation.

notes.title: Point Symmetry
notes.content: A special case of rotational symmetry of order 2 (180-degree rotation).

notes.title: Tessellations
notes.content: Created by repeating symmetric shapes to cover a plane without gaps.

facts.fact: A circle has rotational symmetry around its center for every possible angle.
A scalene triangle has zero lines of symmetry.
The word 'MOM' has a vertical line of symmetry if written in capital letters.
Symmetry in nature, like in a starfish, is often called radial symmetry.
